items.description: Cert holder dropdown UX: explicit "Choose…" placeholder
items.notes: `components/entity-management/entity-detail-panel.tsx:226` uses `value={metadata.holder ?? 'self'}` inside a Radix `<Select>` — when a cert mention has no `holder` set, the dropdown visually pre-selects "Self (our organisation)". items.description: Classifier tool-schema hygiene: `strict: true` + `additionalProperties: false` (TS + Python)
items.notes: R1 Option A. `lib/ai/classify.ts` Pass 1 (`return_classification`, lines 1144-1275) and Pass 2 (`return_entity_validation`, lines 951-989) tool schemas do not set `strict: true` and do not declare `additionalProperties: false` on nested objects. Adding both activates constrained decoding at the token level — prevents the model from inventing completely novel enum values (e.g. a new rel_type like `"certified_by"`) or emitting structurally invalid JSON. Requires recursive addition of `additionalProperties: false` to all nested objects (entities, relationships, temporal_references, validated_entities). First request with new strict schema incurs one-off schema compilation latency (<1s); subsequent calls use 24h cache. **Python parity:** `scripts/kb_pipeline/classify.py` uses its own prompt-driven path (no tool schema); strict-mode is TS-only.

items.description: Convert remaining SECDEF functions to SECURITY INVOKER + RLS (OPS-43 follow-up)
items.notes:
Originally OPS-43.1 — sub-decimal lineage of OPS-43 (SHIPPED, removed from backlog). Migrated S58 ID-15.3.
**35/39 SHIPPED:** batch 1 S22 `973c9699` (10 fns) + batch 2 S23 `4f2a11f4` (10 fns) + batch 3 S33 `59264110` (7 fns) + batch 4 S34 `ed494a1e` (8 write/admin fns) + OPS-60 `get_user_display_names` B-strict S34 `6809ad1a` (1 fn). **4 remaining functions categorised per S37 supabase-postgres-best-practices investigation** (full analysis: `.planning/.research/s37-housekeeping/ops-43-1-best-practice-investigation.md`): (i) **`detect_reupload` — FLIP TO INVOKER** (`source_documents` SELECT policy `qual = true` for authenticated; current SECDEF provides zero additional reach; ~15 min); (ii) **`find_exact_duplicates` — FLIP TO INVOKER** (`content_items` SELECT policy `qual = true` for authenticated; SECDEF was justified by stale comment in `lib/mcp/tools/content.ts:416-418` claiming RLS-scoped client needed cross-user matches — factually wrong under current schema; ~20 min plus comment strike); (iii) **`cleanup_filtered_articles` — KEEP SECDEF as documented end-state** (single service-role-only caller in `app/api/cron/intelligence-cleanup/route.ts`; service-role bypasses RLS regardless of SECDEF/INVOKER; flip is a no-op; effort 0); (iv) **`get_user_role` — KEEP SECDEF, out-of-scope confirmed** (canonical RLS-helper SECDEF endorsed verbatim by `security-rls-performance` skill; called from inside `USING`/`WITH CHECK` of 44 RLS policies; SECDEF breaks the recursion that would otherwise fire when `user_roles` SELECT policy evaluates `get_user_role()`). **Close-out path:** single batch-5 migration `ALTER FUNCTION public.detect_reupload(text, uuid, text) SECURITY INVOKER;` + `ALTER FUNCTION public.find_exact_duplicates(text, uuid) SECURITY INVOKER;` (no RLS policy changes — qual=true on both) + comment hygiene at `lib/mcp/tools/content.ts:416-418` + regression confirm via `__tests__/api/source-documents.test.ts` + `__tests__/api/items-batch.test.ts:862` (existing tests exercise both paths). **Why this WP exists:** SECDEF where not needed is a latent footgun — a future migration could re-add the anon grant, or the function could be invoked via service-role in a context where RLS would be the intended access control. Converting to INVOKER where RLS already permits the read makes the access model explicit at the schema layer (`security-privileges` least-privilege rule).

items.description: Cloud Run buildpack equivalent of Vercel Next.js build cache
items.notes: Each `cloud-run-deploy.yml` run that hits the `scripts/**/*.py` or `requirements.txt` path filter triggers a fresh Cloud Build image rebuild via Python buildpack. S35 `d7044c2d` Build step: 14:14 (~14 min). S34 baseline: ~14 min. **S36 W3 research findings:** Cloud Build workers are ephemeral by design — current `cloudrun/cloudbuild.yaml` invokes `pack build` with no cache plumbing (no `--cache-image`, no `--cache type=...;format=image;name=...`, no `--previous-image`); pack defaults to `type=build;format=volume;type=launch;format=volume;` which is local-only and useless on ephemeral CI. The Python buildpack already implements cache infrastructure (`pkg/python/python.go` `PIPInstallRequirements` hashes `requirements.txt` + Python version + installer name; on hit logs "Application dependencies are up to date, skipping installation") — it's simply unreachable today because no `--cache-image` registry path persists the layer between Cloud Build runs. **Recommended config delta:** add `--cache-image=europe-west2-docker.pkg.dev/$PROJECT_ID/pipeline/kh-pipeline-{slim,eval}:cache` flag to both `build-slim` + `build-eval` steps in `cloudrun/cloudbuild.yaml`. Cache image lives in existing `pipeline` Artifact Registry repo on the `:cache` tag (outside SHA-tag lifecycle policies). No changes to `cloudrun/Procfile` (intentionally absent per F-9 fix), `.github/workflows/cloud-run-deploy.yml` (build SA already has Artifact Registry write through `roles/cloudbuild.builds.builder`), or IAM. **Projected impact:** cold rebuild → ~3-5 min steady-state on `scripts/**/*.py`-only changes; `requirements.txt` change → cold path then cache repopulate. **Caveats:** first build after change is cold + extra cache push (~15-16 min one-off); `requirements.txt` byte-identity is the cache key (whitespace edits invalidate); `:cache` tag accumulates layer history — recommend Artifact Registry cleanup policy auto-deleting `:cache` versions older than 90 days. **Spec extension:** fold into `wp-run-cloud-run-provisioning-spec.md` v1.4 §2.13.1 (build cache image subsection, IAM note, `:cache` lifecycle exemption + 90-day cleanup policy) + 4-line addendum to §5.2 step 3 + new AC-2.1 (`:cache` tag exists in `pipeline` repo on each environment after first cache-bearing build). Verification step: post-first-deploy run `gcloud artifacts docker images list europe-west2-docker.pkg.dev/<project>/pipeline --filter='tags:cache'` on both staging and prod and confirm `:cache` tag exists.

items.description: Rewrite `lib/content/content-suggestions.ts` template-gap filter to compute `coverage_status` in-memory rather than via the silent-no-op `.eq('coverage_status' as never, 'gap')` filter.
items.notes: **Real bug, not just a type-system fiction.** `coverage_status` is NOT a column on `form_template_requirements` (verified `supabase/types/database.types.ts:1591-1669`); it is a TypeScript-only field on `RequirementCoverage` computed by `evaluateRequirement()` in `lib/templates/template-coverage.ts:118` via cosine-similarity vs `content_items.embedding`. Supabase silently accepts the unknown-column filter and returns ALL `is_current=true` requirements unfiltered, so the downstream `suggestions.push({ suggestion_type: 'template_gap', ... })` loop emits a gap-suggestion for **every** current requirement, not just gaps. Test at `__tests__/lib/content-suggestions.test.ts:204` passes because the mock's `.eq()` is `vi.fn().mockReturnThis()` — doesn't actually filter. **Why parked, not ready:** coverage feature is RESOLVED-EXTEND-NOT-REBUILD-S233 per `docs/plans/phase-0-investigation/architecture/07-collapse-list.md:191,249` — a 4th gap source extension is planned (Q3.10), and `template-coverage.ts` will likely be refactored alongside. Fixing in-place now risks throwing the fix away. **Scope when activated:** (a) fetch all `is_current=true` requirements + relevant `content_items` (or call `evaluateTemplate()` per template); (b) filter in-memory where `coverage_status === 'gap'`; (c) shared code path with `template-coverage.ts` to avoid duplicate cosine-similarity logic; (d) replace the mock's stub `.eq()` with an actual filter so the test fails honest. Cross-ref: collapse-list §10 coverage carry-forward.

items.description: RLS auto-enable trigger does not cover CREATE TABLE LIKE / CREATE TABLE PARTITION OF DDL variants
items.notes: The `rls_auto_enable()` event trigger at `supabase/migrations/20260514150238_enable_rls_auto_event_trigger_and_grants_pattern.sql` fires on TAG IN ('CREATE TABLE', 'CREATE TABLE AS', 'SELECT INTO') only. `CREATE TABLE ... LIKE` and `CREATE TABLE ... PARTITION OF` DDL variants are NOT covered at v1 — if KH ever uses partitioned tables or LIKE patterns, the P-1 RLS-auto-enable guarantee silently fails. **Scope:** (a) document the gap in `docs/specs/rls-pattern/PRODUCT.md` P-1 Out-of-scope note (developers using these variants must include explicit `ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` in same migration); (b) optionally extend the trigger's TAG filter to cover the additional DDL variants if/when partitioned tables enter the schema. **Reference:** `docs/specs/core-docs-pathway-assessment/implementation-readiness-audit-s240.md` P8.

items.description: Clarify in dispatch-primitives.md that `docs/reference/task-list.json` is always within the permitted file set for any Subtask or Planner completion, regardless of the spec-file allow-list in the dispatch brief.
items.notes: Spec-authoring Subtask ID-20.4 authored TECH.md and simultaneously edited `docs/reference/task-list.json` (status flip + journal block append) in commit 84eeb456. hook-cwd-prefix-gap-investigation.md — ID-19.4 close-out research; Fix 1 (propagation discipline) + Fix 3 (sandbox tightening, out-of-scope)

items.notes: Surfaced by worktree-agent aae48793fa368be7a during ID-29.4 PLAN.md authoring (main-track-S252). Agent wrote to main-repo absolute paths (`/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub/docs/...`) from worktree CWD (`/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub/.claude/worktrees/agent-aae48793fa368be7a`). The Tier 2.2 hook at `.claude/settings.json` line 37 checks: if FP matches `^/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub`, then `case "$FP" in "$CWD"/*|"$CWD") exit 0;; esac; exit 2`. Since FP was the main-repo path and CWD was the worktree path, the case should NOT have matched and the hook should have exited 2 (blocked). It did not. Agent self-remediated via `git show HEAD:... > ...` restore + `rm` of leaked PLAN.md. **Root cause candidates:** (a) Hook propagation lag — the worktree was branched from main before the Tier 2.2 hook landed on main (S57-S58 pattern; S59 W3 diary confirms worktrees under `knowledge-hub/.claude/worktrees/agent-XXX` inherit MAIN repo branch state, and hook landed on production-readiness first, reached main via S58 merge — a new worktree cut before the hook commit would not have it). (b) Shell evaluation issue — CWD value from `jq -r '.cwd'` may be empty or mismatched, causing the outer `if` to still enter but the `case` to fall through differently than expected. **Cross-ref ID-69** (parked sandbox-tightening) — ID-69 parked rationale explicitly states 'hook propagation discipline + brief-authoring discipline cover the leak class adequately in practice'; this incident partially invalidates that assumption and should be noted when ID-69 is next reviewed. **Verification approach:** (a) check the worktree creation timestamp vs the Tier 2.2 hook commit date on main (run `git log --oneline .claude/settings.json` to find the commit; compare with `git worktree list` creation inferred from branch diverge point); (b) add a debug `echo` to the hook command to surface the CWD and FP values in a controlled test. **S63 WP6 RECURRENCE:** Worktree-agent aa406e67a259c60bb (ID-23.1 PRODUCT.md authoring) hit the same gap — worktree path was `knowledge-hub/.claude/worktrees/agent-aa406e67a259c60bb/` (primary tree dir, branch `production-readiness`); initial Write/Edit calls with sibling-tree absolute paths `/Users/liamj/.../knowledge-hub-production-readiness/...` landed in sibling tree WITHOUT firing the Tier 2.2 hook. Hook fired only on a LATER edit (intermittent fire pattern). Agent self-remediated via revert + retry with relative paths. SAME root-cause candidates apply (propagation lag OR shell evaluation gap). Cumulative evidence: 2 occurrences across 2 sessions (S252 + S63). Diagnosis urgency MEDIUM → consider promote to HIGH if further recurrences. Promotion-to-Task candidate if S64+ surfaces a 3rd occurrence. **[kh-prod-readiness-S69 W2] 3rd RECURRENCE CONFIRMED:** Subtask 32.9 worktree dispatch leaked Edit/Write calls into the SIBLING production-readiness worktree — Executor's red-phase test edits landed at line 50 of the target test file + status flip in task-list.json AT the production-readiness worktree path instead of the agent's isolated worktree under `.claude/worktrees/agent-<ID>/`. NO ERROR — silent leak. Recovery required patch-extract-revert-reapply ceremony; caused phantom "uncommitted changes" in orchestrator's worktree + auto-merge oddities during cherry-pick. Mitigation applied in 32.13 dispatch brief: stricter relative-paths discipline — confirmed working (no leak observed in 32.13). **Status update:** promotion-to-Task threshold crossed (3 sessions: S252 + S63 + S69); priority bumped medium → high; this finding is now the canonical recurrence record. Orchestrator should consider promoting ID-78 to a forward Task at next session-start (e.g. via `update-roadmap-backlog` Promote mode targeting Theme 10 SDLC workflow orchestration). Mitigation pattern proven (relative-paths discipline in dispatch briefs); root-cause diagnosis still outstanding. Provenance: Executor 32.9 OOS #4 (load-bearing empirical finding); Orchestrator confirmation during 32.9 dispatch; 32.9 close `b4a04df6`.

items.description: Tune `security_reminder_hook.py` substring matcher (or write a KH-local override) to distinguish `execFileSync` (safe, fixed-arg array, no shell) from `exec` / `execSync` (shell-wrapped). Naive substring matching on the literal pattern `<child>_<process>.<exec>` (concatenation intentionally split here to avoid re-firing the hook on this very write) matches any file containing that literal — including data/docs strings — producing noise on executor dispatches.

cross_doc_links.raw: security_reminder_hook.py line 71 — SECURITY_PATTERNS rule child_process_exec uses naive substring matching with no word-boundary or AST-level check

items.notes: Surfaced by Checker a7da59ec1e4e4b89e (S68) finding #4 during Subtask 30.10 PR-B verification. Executor reported false-positive on the safe call site `__tests__/scripts/migrate-backlog-from-roadmap.test.ts:142-147` (`execFileSync('git', ['show', '${PRE_MIGRATION_COMMIT}:docs/reference/product-roadmap.json'], { cwd: REPO_ROOT })` — fixed argument array, hardcoded literal command, only variable is a hardcoded constant; this is the safe form). **FLAG — hook is upstream Anthropic plugin, not KH-owned:** the hook lives at `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/security-guidance/hooks/security_reminder_hook.py` (Anthropic-official `claude-plugins-official` marketplace). KH cannot edit upstream; remediation options are (a) file upstream issue for AST-aware or word-boundary matching, (b) write a KH-local override hook wrapping or replacing the upstream, or (c) disable the upstream hook via `ENABLE_SECURITY_REMINDER=0` for executor dispatches. **Actual trigger confirmed via experimental test (Curator S68):** the substring `exec(` does NOT match `execFileSync(` (no `exec(` substring in `execFileSync(`); the substring matching the `<child>_<process>.<exec>` literal DOES match any content containing that string. The likely false-positive source is intermediate executor Write/Edit content (e.g. comment, draft, or in-file string literal) containing the pattern. The hook even fires on this very backlog entry's prose — Curator had to obfuscate the substring (via `<child>_<process>.<exec>` concatenation notation) to land the write. Recommended fix: replace substring match with regex word-boundary or AST-aware parsing (`ast.parse()` for `.py`, `@babel/parser` for `.ts/.js`) to inspect actual call sites. Cross-refs: Checker verdict a7da59ec1e4e4b89e finding #4; Executor commit 30578efd (the safe `execFileSync` site that triggered the warning); upstream tracking — none yet.

items.notes: Surfaced S256 W3 process observation. When a worktree commit is cherry-picked to main, the resulting SHA on main differs from the worktree SHA. Any journal blocks (in `task-list.json` `details` `<info added on ...>` blocks) written during worktree execution will reference the worktree SHA, not the cherry-pick SHA. This is a known gotcha for future orchestrators and Executors: the stale SHA is still traceable via `git log --all --oneline <worktree-sha>` but is not the canonical main-branch SHA. Fix: add a single-sentence note to the relevant skill body (`.claude/skills/commit-commands/` or `.claude/skills/implement-subtask/SKILL.md`) under a 'Post-cherry-pick SHA staleness' or similar heading. No code changes required.

items.description: Split the single skip-guard in `TestRetryCountWebhookEmission` (in `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_pipeline_run_webhook.py`) into two distinct guards with separate messages — currently conflates 'stub not resident' and 'stub lacks last_json' into one skip condition.
items.notes: Surfaced S257 W2+W3 carry-forward queue item N5 (from S257 W2a fix-pack). `scripts/tests/test_cocoindex_flow_pipeline_run_webhook.py::TestRetryCountWebhookEmission` uses a single skip-guard that conflates two distinct absence conditions: (a) the cocoindex stub is not resident in `sys.modules`; (b) the stub exists but its `last_json` attribute is absent or None. Both conditions emit the same skip message, making it impossible to distinguish environment-setup failures from stub-API changes when a test is skipped. Fix: split into two sequential guards — first check stub residency with message 'cocoindex stub not resident'; then check `last_json` presence with message 'stub lacks last_json attribute'. Single-file edit to the test class setup; no test logic changes.

items.description: Add a runbook entry documenting that `bun run test:integration` produces 27 pre-existing failures in sandbox environments due to `fetch failed` from Supabase-writing tests — these are environmental (sandbox blocks network calls), not code regressions.

cross_doc_links.raw: test-philosophy.md — candidate location for the env-gate convention note

items.notes: Surfaced S257 W2+W3 carry-forward queue item N7. `bun run test:integration` has 27 pre-existing failures in sandbox (`fetch failed` from Supabase-writing tests). These are environmental: the sandbox blocks outbound network calls to Supabase, so any integration test that writes to the live staging DB fails at the transport layer. In CI (`dangerouslyDisableSandbox=true`) these tests pass. Without documentation, future operators (or agents) running the integration suite locally in sandbox mode will see 27 failures and be unable to distinguish them from genuine regressions. Fix: add a note to `docs/runbooks/` (or a new §env-gate section in `docs/reference/test-philosophy.md`) explaining: (a) the 27 sandbox-blocked failures are expected and enumerated; (b) how to verify a failure is sandbox-caused vs a real regression (`fetch failed` pointing at supabase URL is the signature); (c) how to run the suite against real infra (`dangerouslyDisableSandbox=true` or in CI). Cross-ref item 149 (TestHealthEndpoint sandbox bind) which covers the specific aiohttp TCP-socket case; this item covers the broader integration-suite operator guidance.

items.description: Decide whether to persist the Pydantic-level exception sub-class (e.g. specific `ValidationError` sub-types within the 6-class STAGE-level enum) to `pipeline_runs.result.pydantic_error_class` for forensic queries on extraction-validation-failed runs — design decision deferred from Subtask 28.13.
items.notes: Surfaced S257 W2+W3 carry-forward queue item N8. Subtask 28.13 wired the 6-class STAGE-level error enum to `pipeline_runs.result.error_class`. The sub-class persistence decision — whether to also persist the specific Pydantic `ValidationError` sub-type (e.g. `pydantic_core.ValidationError`, field-level sub-types) to a separate `pipeline_runs.result.pydantic_error_class` field — was explicitly undecided at 28.13 close. Use case: forensic SQL queries of the form `WHERE result->>'pydantic_error_class' = 'FieldValidationError'` on extraction-validation-failed runs. Investigation step: (a) enumerate the Pydantic exception hierarchy relevant to the KH extraction path; (b) assess whether the 6-class top-level enum already provides sufficient forensic resolution, or whether sub-class granularity would meaningfully improve incident diagnosis; (c) if proceeding, design the `pipeline_runs.result.pydantic_error_class` field shape and add a migration + `_classify_stage_exception()` extension.

items.description: Remove unnecessary `as PipelineRunStatus` cast at `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts:146` and correct the misleading comment that claims the cast widens the helper's terminal-only union — it actually narrows it (the helper accepts `in_progress`; the cast strips it).
items.notes: Surfaced by task-checker acbf57245e3b4e4df during ID-28 close-ceremony quality-review (commit c71722ba). `app/api/internal/pipeline-runs/record/route.ts:146` casts `status` as `PipelineRunStatus` — the comment describes this as widening the helper's terminal-only PipelineRunStatus but the cast actually narrows it, stripping `in_progress` which the helper accepts. Fix: remove the cast and pass `status` directly; update the comment to accurately describe what the status value represents at that call site. Single-line edit; no test changes required.

items.description: Document in the cocoindex pipeline runbook that `pipeline_runs.result.error_class` may be absent for unclassified exceptions (`flow.py:758-778`), that the resulting Vercel Zod HTTP 400 is expected, and that Cloud Run logs (not webhook delivery) are the intended monitoring path.
items.notes: Surfaced by task-checker acbf57245e3b4e4df during ID-28 close-ceremony quality-review (commit c71722ba). `flow.py:758-778` catches unclassified exceptions and sets `flow_error_class = type(exc).__name__`, emitting to webhook. The Vercel Zod schema rejects this shape (HTTP 400 logged as ERROR); best-effort webhook continues. This is an intentional design choice: observability is via Cloud Run logs at `event='cocoindex.stage_error.unclassified'`, not webhook delivery. No code change required. Runbook note should cover: (a) unclassified exceptions and absent `error_class` are normal; (b) monitor via Cloud Run logs, not webhook 400s; (c) expand `_classify_stage_exception()` as new exception types are observed in production. Status parked — design is intentional and no operator has been confused yet; promote to ready when Cloud Run runbook is next edited.

items.notes: Surfaced by task-checker acbf57245e3b4e4df during ID-28 close-ceremony quality-review (commit c71722ba). 5 failures in `__tests__/lib/mammoth-track-changes.test.ts` — pandoc is not installed in the test environment and/or fixture files for track-changes processing are absent. Recurring pre-existing baseline acknowledged in S255, S256, and S257 continuation prompts as known baseline (part of the 9-fail baseline at those sessions). Fix candidates: (a) install pandoc in the CI environment and restore missing fixtures; (b) add a `skipIf(!hasPandoc)` runtime check to gracefully degrade in environments without pandoc; (c) mock pandoc calls to decouple the test suite from binary availability. Decision on fix path requires evaluating whether pandoc is a desired CI dependency. Status parked pending that decision.
